options: The representative root of x's group
The number of nodes connected to x
A list of all of x's direct neighbors
True if x has already been visited
items.answer: The representative root of x's group
items.explanation_md: `find` walks the `parent` chain up to the root that represents x's whole group. Two nodes are in the same group exactly when their roots match.

items.prompt_md: You're given an adjacency matrix of direct connections between n cities and must count how many groups of indirectly-connected cities exist. Which approach fits?
options: Union-Find: union every direct connection, then count distinct roots
Topological sort to find a valid ordering of the cities
Multi-source BFS seeded from every city at once
Binary search over the number of groups
items.answer: Union-Find: union every direct connection, then count distinct roots
items.explanation_md: "Merge pairwise connections, then ask how many groups" is the Union-Find fingerprint — union each connected pair, then the number of distinct roots is the number of groups.

items.prompt_md: With path compression, what is the effective (amortized) time complexity of each `find` or `union` call?
options: Nearly O(1) — O(α(n)), the inverse Ackermann function
items.answer: Nearly O(1) — O(α(n)), the inverse Ackermann function
items.explanation_md: Path compression flattens the tree every time `find` walks it, so repeated calls converge to amortized O(α(n)) — a function that grows so slowly it's effectively constant for any realistic n.
